page: Amazon Web Services x2idn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 2048.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 16.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 3800 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieved a top-tier PassMark CPU score of 87072.06 and a strong stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 123819.6. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 330.0 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 136.46 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Database operations are also a strength, with PassMark database operations scoring 30075.87 and Redis SET operations reaching 105.14 million operations per second. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1890.39.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the x2idn.32xlarge offers high resource density, combining massive memory capacity with high-speed local NVMe storage and 100 Gbps network bandwidth. This profile is highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and data-intensive enterprise applications. While it performs well in multi-threaded tasks and prompt processing for small to medium language models, it exhibits weak performance in text generation for large 70B models due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware acceleration.
description: The Amazon Web Services x2idn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. It features 128 vCPUs powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 2048.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and a 3800 GB local NVMe SSD. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU capabilities and strong memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds reaching 330.0 GB/sec and Redis SET operations exceeding 105 million operations per second. While single-core performance and large-scale LLM text generation are limited, its high memory-to-core ratio and fast local storage make it highly efficient for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data-intensive applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2idn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 2048 GB DDR4 RAM, 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for demanding database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2idn.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 2048 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, fast local NVMe storage, and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs powered by dedicated 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processors
2048.0 GB of high-speed DDR4 memory (16.0 GB per core)
Bundled 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage volume
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data-intensive workloads
